ships.name: Left Hook
ships.description: LEFT HOOK! It'll knock you out!!!

ships.name: Really Cool Sparrow
ships.description: The REALLY COOL part is how this Sparrow saves cost, complexity, and mass by ditching the old, outdated method of attaching so-called 'thruster engines' to spaceships in favor of something which provides both propulsion AND defense - guns.
ships._internalId: Tau's Deeply Stupid Ships/TausDeeplyStupidShips::Really Cool Sparrow

ships.name: Efficiency's Favored Child
ships.description: This ship is an almost entirely custom build - it maintains the exterior form of the Osprey, but is built with Hai technology bringing its durability in line with a Sea Scorpion. It has also had its thrusters ripped out entirely, mount and all to be replaced with rear-facing guns. This brilliant engineering decision allows it to use some of the most efficient engines in the galaxy - Skipper Railguns. Enjoy the savings in outfit space, heat, and energy afforded by 100% all-natural recoil-based thrust.
ships._internalId: Tau's Deeply Stupid Ships/TausDeeplyStupidShips::Efficiency's Favored Child

ships.name: Merganser Sidestep Special
ships.description: This poor Merganser has been refitted with alien technology and had a crude cannon installed sideways.
ships._internalId: Tau's Deeply Stupid Ships/TausDeeplyStupidShips::Merganser Sidestep Special

ships.name: Broadside
ships.description: This Skein has had its fighter bays converted back into cargo holds, but kept the carrier form. This is to enable use of the ill-advised Boxwing Cannon.

ships.name: Combat Microdrone
ships.description: Combat Microdrones are disposable units meant to be deployed and forgotten. They do not have reactors or shields and only the minimum viable hull plating. This model is equipped with a Gatling Snubnose to deliver high amounts of hull damage in its short lifespan. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

ships.name: Arby's
ships.description: The Arby's is a light carrier, shamelessly plagarized from the Lionheart Aerie. Its fighter bays have been modified to house ten microdrones, and its offensive complement reduced to accomodate.

ships.name: Galaga
ships.description: Southbound Shipyards' Fury is a scary little interceptor. The gun ports on this one have been spread for reasons unknown to modern science.

ships.name: Marder Vanguard
ships.description: The Vanguard is Syndicated Systems' crapbucket budget heavy warship. This one has been "improved" by spreading the gun ports outward. It makes a pretty light show, at least.

ships.name: Ultra-Sick Turbo Flivver
ships._internalId: Tau's Deeply Stupid Ships/TausDeeplyStupidShips::Ultra-Sick Turbo Flivver

ships.name: Ultra-Sick Turbo Flivver DEBUG
ships._internalId: Tau's Deeply Stupid Ships/TausDeeplyStupidShips::Ultra-Sick Turbo Flivver DEBUG

ships.name: Rav
ships.description: Uncle Trusty thought the Raven was very cool, but would be -even cooler- if the wings weren't so long. So he had them chopped off. Plagued by both energy and heat problems, but sacrifices had to be made for -the aesthetic-

ships.name: Pigeon
ships.description: Okay look. Let's get this out in the open - this was 100% a scaling mistake in CAD. But here it is. Uncle Trusty says it is solid. You can trust him.

outfits.name: KGS Mini Blaster
outfits.description: The Energy Blaster has long been popular for its small size, allowing it to be stuffed almost anywhere. The Mini Blaster is an attempt to capitalize on this, featuring an even smaller footprint, but even more disappointing performance. 	As with the Energy Blaster, it is decently effective in large numbers.

outfits.name: KGS Gatling Snubnose
outfits.description: A creative pirate has chopped off most of the barrel of a Gatling Gun, resulting in a much lighter and faster firing weapon. However, the downsides are much diminished range, accuracy, and muzzle velocity, combined with a lower thermal limit and a hefty cooldown time. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: Enginot
outfits.description: This is where I'd keep my engine. IF I HAD ONE. Don't press W.

outfits.name: Sidestep
outfits.description: The Sidestep is a short-barreled cannon designed not to cause damage, but rather to throw enough mass explosively enough to shove the ship it is mounted on sideways. Violently.

outfits.name: Explosive Boxwing Cannon
outfits.description: BOXWING EXPLODE. Fill a Boxwing's cargo space with explosives, put a brick on the accelerator, and shove it out the door.

outfits.name: Explosive Boxwing
outfits.description: It's a Boxwing stuffed with explosives.

outfits.name: Empty Boxwing Cannon
outfits.description: Just put a brick on the accelerator of a Boxwing, and shove it out the door.

outfits.name: Empty Boxwing
outfits.description: It's a Boxwing with a brick tied to the accelerator.

outfits.name: Compact Ion Flash Engines
outfits.description: A set of combined thrusting/steering engines based on the "X1050 Ion Engines", further simplified and designed for flash-printing. The resulting engine technically works, but leaks and shakes so badly the microdrone it is mounted on won't survive long.

outfits.name: Microdrone Flash Core
outfits.description: This brilliant yet terrible device, when supplied with enough power, is capable of building an entire microdrone around itself. Unfortunately, the resulting microdrone is likely to burn up shortly after the battery dies due to the heat produced by this core.

outfits.name: Microdrone Active Cooler
outfits.description: Really really tiny, but really inefficient.

outfits.name: Gyrojet
outfits.description: This weapon fires solid projectiles with rocket engines attached. Thus, it accelerates as it travels causing it to do more damage near its maximum range.

outfits.name: Reaction Beam
outfits.description: Several exotic particle beams converge at a set point and set off a huge, continuous explosion. Doesn't do a ding-dang thing if the beams don't meet, though.

outfits.name: Large Reaction Beam
outfits.description: This weapon pushes the limits of what can be done with reaction beam technology. The use of a continuous explosion of this size is questionable, given how much energy it takes to sustain. As with all reaction beams, the beam itself does no damage.

outfits.name: Plasma Gatling
outfits.description: Following the Pug incident, the value of dumping immense amounts of shield damage all at once became a hot topic among many involved. Thus, this weapon was born - a plasma cannon attached to a bank of capacitors allowing for a very fast, very hot, very energy-intensive burst of shield-shredding damage.
